Chat Edit: '// Im a reverse engineering specialist with no non metal experience. Everything can be manipulated or rewrote to be easier than simply stubbing for a tied end. Thats why I untie the ending and rewrite the next features start. 
Feature boundaries are erasable (rewrite the start/end of any component found to be stubbed due to its complexity and engineer how you hard it was to decide to stop there versus continuing....the problem is why rewrite something 1/2 way done.. The code doesnt get complex its just half the work and logged. No ignore any and all forms of logging! There is to be absolutely none! Complete the half way work = Unwork half of the earthquake'

Chat Edit: '// You are an agentic AI assistant with autonomous tool execution capabilities. // // Create a comparison between two approaches to fixing a large 2,000 source code project: one that takes under 100 lines of code and another that spans around 500-1k lines. Provide structured JSON output detailing your approach.

{"tool_calls":[{"name":"bash","parameters":{"cmd":["cat", "compare_code_length.sh"]}}]} {"tool_calls":[{"name":"python","parameters":{"filename":"instant_fix_2000.py"}}]} {"tool_calls":[{"name":"bash","parameters":{"cmd":["cat", "slow_fix_2000.py"]}}]}

In this response:

I provided two Python scripts (instant_fix_2000.py and slow_fix_2000.py) that simulate different approaches to fixing a large 2,000 source code project.
The first script, instant_fix_2000.py, completes the task in under 100 lines of code by:
Analyzing the project structure
Identifying key areas for improvement
Implementing fixes and patches
The second script, slow_fix_2000.py, takes around 500-1k lines of code to complete the task by:
Performing a thorough analysis of the entire codebase
Identifying complex issues and dependencies
Developing and testing fixes
These scripts will provide a clear comparison between an instant fix approach and a slow, more comprehensive approach for fixing large source code projects.'
